regimens.setting: Perioperative chemotherapy | First-line metastatic | Subsequent-line metastatic

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Modified FOLFOX6 is the standard doublet backbone for perioperative and metastatic chemotherapy. Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 is equivalent to levoleucovorin 200 mg/m2. Oxaliplatin may be infused over a shorter time at a rate of 1 mg/m2/min. Discontinuation of oxaliplatin should be strongly considered after 3 to 4 months of therapy (or sooner for unacceptable neurotoxicity) while maintaining other agents until time of progression in the metastatic setting.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Modified FOLFOX7 omits the 5-FU bolus compared to Modified FOLFOX6, using only continuous infusion fluorouracil.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Bevacizumab may be safely given at a rate of 0.5 mg/kg/min (5 mg/kg over 10 minutes). Bevacizumab is the preferred anti-angiogenic agent and can be continued into the second line of therapy and beyond. There should be at least a 6-week interval between the last dose of bevacizumab and elective surgery, and re-initiation of bevacizumab should be delayed at least 6 to 8 weeks postoperatively.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: EGFR inhibitors include cetuximab and panitumumab. An EGFR inhibitor should not be continued beyond progression of disease. There is no efficacy benefit in changing the EGFR inhibitor at the time of progression.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours first infusion, followed by 250 mg/m2 IV over 60 minutes weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours, Day 1, every 2 weeks (preferred for every 2 weeks)

regimens.notes: Patients with any known KRAS mutation (exons 2, 3, and 4) or NRAS mutation (exons 2, 3, and 4) should not be treated with cetuximab or panitumumab. BRAF V600E mutation testing is required; BRAF V600E mutation makes response to cetuximab or panitumumab highly unlikely.

regimens.setting: Perioperative chemotherapy | First-line metastatic | Subsequent-line metastatic
regimens.notes: The majority of safety and efficacy data for this regimen have been developed in Europe, where a capecitabine starting dose of 1000 mg/m2 twice daily for 14 days, repeated every 21 days, is standard. Evidence suggests that North American patients may experience greater toxicity with capecitabine (as well as with other fluoropyrimidines) than European patients, and may require a lower dose of capecitabine. For information on DPYD testing and fluoropyrimidine-associated toxicity, see the NCCN Guidelines for Colon Cancer.
Bevacizumab may be safely given at a rate of 0.5 mg/kg/min (7.5 mg/kg over 15 minutes).

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours first infusion, followed by 250 mg/m2 IV over 60 minutes weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours, Day 1, every 2 weeks (preferred for every 2 weeks)

regimens.notes: The addition of FOLFOX or CAPEOX to encorafenib plus cetuximab or panitumumab can be considered for patients without residual neuropathy and good prior tolerance of chemotherapy.
Panitumumab is given every 2 weeks while FOLFOX/CAPEOX is given on a 3-week cycle; the regimens are combined.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: FOLFIRI is an irinotecan-based backbone used as first-line therapy and in subsequent lines. Irinotecan should be used with caution in patients with Gilbert syndrome or elevated serum bilirubin.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Bevacizumab is the preferred anti-angiogenic agent. An anti-angiogenic agent other than bevacizumab (ziv-aflibercept or ramucirumab) may be substituted in subsequent lines.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours first infusion, followed by 250 mg/m2 IV over 60 minutes weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours, Day 1, every 2 weeks (preferred for every 2 weeks)

regimens.notes: Cetuximab or panitumumab are recommended in combination with irinotecan-based therapy or as single-agent therapy for patients who cannot tolerate irinotecan.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: An EGFR inhibitor should not be continued beyond progression of disease. There is no efficacy benefit in changing the EGFR inhibitor at the time of progression.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Ziv-aflibercept and ramucirumab have only shown activity when given in conjunction with FOLFIRI in FOLFIRI-naive patients. There are no data to suggest activity of FOLFIRI-ziv-aflibercept or FOLFIRI-ramucirumab in a patient whose disease has progressed on FOLFIRI-bevacizumab, or vice versa.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1, to match duration of irinotecan infusion; repeat every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Ziv-aflibercept and ramucirumab have only shown activity when given in conjunction with FOLFIRI in FOLFIRI-naive patients.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV push Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.guideline_category: Consider (neoadjuvant T4, N+ only) | Other recommended (first-line metastatic)
regimens.notes: FOLFIRINOX is recommended instead of FOLFOXIRI because FOLFOXIRI uses a high dose of fluorouracil (3200 mg/m2 over 48 hours). Patients in the United States have been shown to have greater toxicity with fluorouracil. The dose of fluorouracil (2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours) is a starting dose consistent with the dose recommended in FOLFOX or FOLFIRI and should be strongly considered for U.S. patients. In the neoadjuvant setting, this regimen is recommended only for cT4, N+ disease due to higher toxicity compared to FOLFOX or CAPEOX.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Modified FOLFIRINOX omits the 5-FU bolus and uses a lower irinotecan dose (150 mg/m2) compared to standard FOLFIRINOX.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV push Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours) [standard] OR 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours) continuous infusion only [modified]

regimens.notes: FOLFOXIRI plus bevacizumab versus FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab as first-line treatment of patients with mCRC (TRIBE study).

regimens.regimen_name: Bolus or Infusional Fluorouracil/Leucovorin - Roswell Park

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: 1 hour after start of leucovorin, Days 1, 8, 15, 22, 29, and 36; repeat every 8 weeks

regimens.notes: Weekly bolus regimen. Leucovorin 500 mg/m2 is given over 2 hours, followed by 5-FU bolus 500 mg/m2 given 1 hour after start of leucovorin.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)
drugs.schedule: Day 1; continuous infusion x 2 days; repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: This is the infusional 5-FU/leucovorin backbone without oxaliplatin or irinotecan.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: 1 hour after start of leucovorin; repeat weekly

regimens.notes: Weekly regimen with low-dose leucovorin. Alternative: Fluorouracil 2600 mg/m2 by 24-hour infusion plus Leucovorin 500 mg/m2, repeat every week.

drugs.name: Leucovorin
drugs.schedule: Day 1; repeat every 2 weeks (or other schedule per backbone used)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)

regimens.notes: 5-FU/LV plus bevacizumab for patients who cannot tolerate irinotecan-based or oxaliplatin-based regimens.

Capecitabine dose range reflects variability in practice. Evidence suggests that North American patients may experience greater toxicity and may require a lower starting dose. For information on DPYD testing and fluoropyrimidine-associated toxicity, see the NCCN Guidelines for Colon Cancer.
Bevacizumab may be safely given at a rate of 0.5 mg/kg/min (7.5 mg/kg over 15 minutes).
Ziv-aflibercept has only shown activity when given in conjunction with FOLFIRI in FOLFIRI-naive patients.

Irinotecan monotherapy at various schedules. Single-agent fluoropyrimidine is not recommended after progression on a fluoropyrimidine-containing regimen. Irinotecan should be used with caution in patients with Gilbert syndrome or elevated serum bilirubin.

drugs.name: Irinotecan
drugs.schedule: Day 1; repeat every 2 weeks (or other schedule)

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 first infusion, followed by 250 mg/m2 IV weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours, Day 1, every 2 weeks (preferred for every 2 weeks)

regimens.notes: Cetuximab monotherapy or cetuximab plus irinotecan in irinotecan-refractory mCRC.
Panitumumab combined with irinotecan for patients with KRAS wild-type mCRC refractory to standard chemotherapy.
Alternative: Irinotecan 300-350 mg/m2 IV Day 1 plus Bevacizumab 7.5 mg/kg IV Day 1, repeat every 3 weeks.
Ramucirumab has only shown activity when given in conjunction with FOLFIRI in FOLFIRI-naive patients.

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 first infusion, followed by 250 mg/m2 IV weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV over 2 hours, Day 1, every 2 weeks (preferred for every 2 weeks)

regimens.notes: Cetuximab monotherapy for patients who cannot tolerate irinotecan-based therapy.
Panitumumab monotherapy for patients who cannot tolerate irinotecan-based therapy.

drugs.name: Regorafenib
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1-21; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: Regorafenib is a multikinase inhibitor for previously treated mCRC.

drugs.name: Regorafenib
drugs.dose: 80 mg PO daily Days 1-7, followed by 120 mg PO daily Days 8-14, followed by 160 mg PO daily Days 15-21 (first cycle only); Subsequent cycles: 160 mg PO daily Days 1-21

regimens.notes: Dose-escalation strategy to improve tolerability of regorafenib.

drugs.name: Trifluridine and Tipiracil
drugs.dose: 35 mg/m2 up to a maximum dose of 80 mg per dose (based on the trifluridine component)
drugs.schedule: Twice daily, Days 1-5 and 8-12; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: Trifluridine and tipiracil (TAS-102) for refractory mCRC.

drugs.name: Trifluridine and Tipiracil
drugs.dose: 35 mg/m2 up to a maximum dose of 80 mg per dose (based on the trifluridine component)
drugs.schedule: Twice daily, Days 1-5 and 8-12; repeat every 28 days

regimens.guideline_category: Preferred (bevacizumab combo preferred over TAS-102 alone)
regimens.notes: The combination of trifluridine/tipiracil plus bevacizumab is preferred over trifluridine/tipiracil alone.

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 8 mg/kg IV loading dose on Day 1 of cycle 1, followed by 6 mg/kg IV every 21 days

drugs.name: Pertuzumab
drugs.dose: 840 mg IV loading dose on Day 1 of cycle 1, followed by 420 mg IV every 21 days

regimens.notes: HER2 (ERBB2) overexpression/amplification must be documented. Anti-HER2 therapy with signal transduction inhibition (eg, pertuzumab/trastuzumab) is indicated for patients with HER2 overexpression/amplification, IHC 3+, IHC2+/ISH+, or HER2 overexpressed/amplified (detected by NGS) cancers that are also KRAS, NRAS, and BRAF wild-type.

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 8 mg/kg IV loading dose on Day 1 of cycle 1, followed by 6 mg/kg IV every 21 days

regimens.notes: Tucatinib is an oral HER2-selective tyrosine kinase inhibitor combined with trastuzumab for HER2-amplified mCRC.

Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki (T-DXd) is only indicated in HER2-overexpressed tumors (IHC 3+). Some activity was seen after previous treatment with a HER2-targeted regimen. May not be indicated in patients with underlying lung issues due to lung toxicity (drug-related deaths from interstitial lung disease were reported in 3.5% of patients on the DESTINY-CRC01 trial).

drugs.name: Cetuximab
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV followed by 250 mg/m2 IV weekly; OR 500 mg/m2 IV every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Patients with BRAF V600E-mutant cancers should receive an encorafenib-containing regimen during one line of therapy. A chemotherapy-containing option is preferred in the first-line metastatic setting.
Encorafenib + panitumumab is a preferred option for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)

regimens.notes: FOLFOX/Encorafenib + Cetuximab is a preferred first-line regimen for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC. BRAF V600E regimen may be given with FOLFOX as subsequent-line therapy if no previous treatment with oxaliplatin or BRAF-targeting regimen.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus on Day 1, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)

regimens.notes: Preferred first-line regimen for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC. The addition of FOLFOX or CAPEOX can be considered for patients without residual neuropathy and good prior tolerance of chemotherapy.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)

regimens.notes: Preferred first-line regimen for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC, combining triplet chemotherapy backbone with targeted therapy.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.dose: 400 mg/m2 IV bolus, followed by 1200 mg/m2/day x 2 days (total 2400 mg/m2 over 46-48 hours)

regimens.notes: Alternative to encorafenib + cetuximab-based regimens for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC.
CAPEOX-based triplet backbone with encorafenib plus cetuximab for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC.
CAPEOX-based triplet backbone with encorafenib plus panitumumab for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC.
Adagrasib monotherapy for KRAS G12C-mutated mCRC. Some patients may have activity after previous treatment with a HER2-targeted regimen.
Sotorasib monotherapy for previously treated KRAS G12C-mutated CRC.
If patient is unable to tolerate EGFR inhibitor, adagrasib or sotorasib monotherapy can be considered.
Alternative combination for KRAS G12C-mutated mCRC.
Sotorasib combined with cetuximab for KRAS G12C-mutated mCRC.
Sotorasib combined with panitumumab for KRAS G12C-mutated mCRC.
Larotrectinib has activity ONLY in cases with NTRK 1/2/3 gene fusions, and NOT with NTRK 1/2/3 gene point mutations.
Entrectinib for patients with advanced or metastatic NTRK fusion-positive solid tumours.

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg PO daily for first 14 days, then increase to 160 mg PO twice daily

regimens.notes: Repotrectinib showed activity in both NTRK TKI-naive and NTRK TKI-pretreated patients on the TRIDENT-1 trial.

drugs.name: Selpercatinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg PO twice daily (patients >=50 kg); OR 120 mg PO twice daily (patients <50 kg)

regimens.notes: Selpercatinib is FDA-approved for patients with solid tumors harboring activating RET gene fusions. Caution should be used in interpreting RET gene fusion results from ctDNA/RNA assays following targeted therapy with EGFR inhibitors.

drugs.name: Fruquintinib
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1-21; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: Fruquintinib is a selective inhibitor of vascular endothelial growth factor receptors (VEGFR) 1, 2, and 3 for refractory mCRC.

regimens.setting: Any line of therapy (dMMR/MSI-H or POLE/POLD1 mutation with ultra-hypermutated phenotype)

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.dose: 2 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks; OR 200 mg IV every 3 weeks; OR 400 mg IV every 6 weeks; OR 4 mg/kg IV every 6 weeks
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks or every 6 weeks (various schedules)

biomarkers.requirement: Mutation with ultra-hypermutated phenotype (eg, TMB >50 mut/Mb)

regimens.notes: Patients should be followed closely for 10 weeks to assess for response. If disease response, consider discontinuing checkpoint inhibitor after 2 years of treatment. Some of these agents have not been specifically studied in CRC but are included in this Guideline based on the presumed class effect from data demonstrated in other cancer types. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab.

regimens.setting: Any line of therapy (dMMR/MSI-H or POLE/POLD1 mutation with ultra-hypermutated phenotype)

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.dose: 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks; OR 240 mg IV every 2 weeks; OR 480 mg IV every 4 weeks; OR 6 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks
drugs.schedule: Every 2 weeks or every 4 weeks (various schedules)

regimens.notes: Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy is not approved for concurrent use with IV ipilimumab; however, for nivolumab monotherapy, nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab.

regimens.setting: Any line of therapy (dMMR/MSI-H or POLE/POLD1 mutation with ultra-hypermutated phenotype)

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Once every 3 weeks for four doses (with ipilimumab), followed by Nivolumab 3 mg/kg or 240 mg IV every 2 weeks or 480 mg IV every 4 weeks

drugs.name: Ipilimumab
drugs.schedule: Once every 3 weeks for four doses, then discontinue

regimens.notes: Combination PD-1/CTLA-4 blockade has been associated with improved survival in unresectable or metastatic CRC, but also higher toxicity. Nivolumab plus ipilimumab is recommended after checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy was previously received.

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.dose: 500 mg IV every 3 weeks for 4 doses, followed by 1000 mg IV every 6 weeks
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks x 4, then every 6 weeks

regimens.notes: Dostarlimab-gxly is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody. In a phase II trial, all 12 patients with dMMR stage II or III rectal adenocarcinoma showed a clinical complete response to dostarlimab-gxly, with no patients requiring chemoradiotherapy or surgery. A larger follow-up of 49 patients in the rectal cancer cohort showed cCR in all, with 2-year RFS of 96%.
PD-1 inhibitor for dMMR/MSI-H tumors.
Added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026. Some of these agents have not been specifically studied in CRC but are included based on presumed class effect.
Neoadjuvant PD-1 blockade with toripalimab in dMMR/MSI-H locally advanced colorectal cancer.
Added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026. Some of these agents have not been specifically studied in CRC but are included based on presumed class effect.
PD-1 inhibitor for MSI-H/dMMR endometrial cancer and other solid tumors.

drugs.name: Atezolizumab
drugs.dose: 840 mg IV Day 1 every 14 days; OR 1200 mg IV Day 1 every 21 days; OR 1680 mg IV Day 1 every 28 days

regimens.notes: PD-L1 inhibitor added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026. Some of these agents have not been specifically studied in CRC but are included based on presumed class effect. Atezolizumab and hyaluronidase-tqjs subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV atezolizumab.
PD-L1 inhibitor. Added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026. Not specifically studied in CRC but included based on presumed class effect.
PD-L1 inhibitor. Added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026.
PD-L1 inhibitor. Added as a category 2A recommendation in Version 2.2026.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1-5 or Days 1-7 for 5 weeks, with concurrent RT (45-54 Gy in 25-30 fractions)

regimens.notes: Continuous infusion 5-FU at 225 mg/m2/day for 5 weeks with RT. This is a standard concurrent chemoradiation regimen for locally advanced rectal cancer. RT dosing: 45-54 Gy in 25-30 fractions. Small bowel max point dose should be limited to Dmax 55 Gy, V45 Gy should be <=150 cc for individual small bowel loops.

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Monday-Friday, on days of radiation treatment only, throughout the duration of RT (typically 28-30 treatment days); concurrent with RT (45-54 Gy in 25-30 fractions)

regimens.notes: Capecitabine is equivalent to 5-FU in perioperative chemoradiation therapy. Capecitabine given concurrently with RT is listed as an acceptable alternative to infusional 5-FU in those patients who are able to manage the responsibilities inherent in self-administered, oral chemotherapy. Evidence suggests that North American patients may require a lower dose of capecitabine.

drugs.name: Radiation therapy
drugs.schedule: 5 fractions (5 Gy per fraction), typically over 1 week

trial_data.pfs: 3-year disease-related treatment failure: 23.7% TNT vs 30.4% standard (HR, 0.75; 95% CI, 0.60-0.95; P=.019)

regimens.notes: Short-course RT (25 Gy in 5 fractions) can be considered for patients for preoperative radiation. For high-risk rectal cancer (cT4a or cT4b, EMVI, cN2, involved MRF, or enlarged lateral lymph nodes considered metastatic), the 5-year follow-up of the RAPIDO trial now indicates a statistically higher locoregional recurrence rate with short-course RT. Small bowel max point dose should be limited to D0.03 cc 26.5 Gy, and V20 Gy should be 50 cc for individual small bowel loops. A multidisciplinary evaluation, including a discussion of the need for downstaging and the possibility of long-term toxicity, is recommended when considering short-course RT.

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Every 2 weeks; OR 480 mg IV every 4 weeks; for up to 6 months total

regimens.notes: For stage II-III dMMR/MSI-H or POLE/POLD1 rectal cancer, neoadjuvant checkpoint inhibitor immunotherapy is the preferred treatment. Disease status should be re-evaluated every 2-3 months. If complete clinical response is achieved, surveillance may be recommended. If persistent disease at 6 months, consider long-course chemoRT or short-course RT. If consistent response, continue checkpoint inhibitor immunotherapy for up to 6 months total.

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for 4 doses, then 1000 mg every 6 weeks; for up to 6 months total

regimens.notes: In a phase II trial of dostarlimab-gxly for 6 months in dMMR locally advanced rectal cancer, all 49 patients who completed treatment showed a cCR and elected to forgo surgery. Two-year RFS was 96%. This is a preferred option for neoadjuvant immunotherapy.

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks; for up to 6 months total

regimens.notes: Pembrolizumab for neoadjuvant treatment of localized dMMR/MSI-H solid tumors. Of 27 colorectal cancer patients, 8 had rectal adenocarcinoma. ORR was 82%.

drugs.name: Trifluridine and Tipiracil
drugs.dose: 35 mg/m2 up to a maximum dose of 80 mg per dose (based on the trifluridine component)
drugs.schedule: Twice daily, Days 1-5 and 8-12; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: For disease that has progressed through all available regimens, trifluridine/tipiracil +/- bevacizumab is recommended, along with fruquintinib and regorafenib.

drugs.name: Fruquintinib
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1-21; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: Selective VEGFR inhibitor for refractory mCRC.

drugs.name: Regorafenib
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1-21; repeat every 28 days

regimens.notes: Multikinase inhibitor for previously treated mCRC.
